title: RefraktConfig
description: Project-level configuration for refrakt.md. Three valid shapes — flat (legacy, deprecated in v0.12, slated for removal in v1.0), singular `site`, plural `sites` — all collapse to a canonical normalized form internally. The unversioned URL https://refrakt.md/refrakt.config.schema.json is a 'latest' alias that always serves the most recently published schema.

plugins.description: Plugins for this project — npm packages that contribute runes, layouts, pipeline hooks, behaviors, and/or CLI commands and MCP tools. When set, this is authoritative; when absent, plugin discovery falls back to scanning package.json for @refrakt-md/* dependencies.

sites.description: Multi-site declaration keyed by site name. Mutually exclusive with `site`.

theme.description: Legacy shorthand for sites.default.theme. Accepts either a package name string (legacy shorthand) or a full SiteThemeConfig object with `package`, `presets`, `tokens`, `modes`, and `code.colorScheme`.

target.description: Legacy shorthand for sites.default.target. Documentation-only — no adapter consumes this field. Slated for removal in v1.0.

backgrounds.description: Named bg presets (keyed by name). Each is a BgPresetDefinition: `style` and/or token-driven `gradient` (engine-resolved), `extends` a base preset, and/or `sandbox` ({ src, framework?, dependencies? }) for a live sandbox backdrop resolved at transform time (SPEC-104). `sandbox` presets are project-level content.

repoUrl.description: Legacy shorthand for sites.default.repoUrl. Canonical GitHub (or compatible) repository URL — e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo`. Used by the `file-ref` rune to build deep-link `View source` URLs.

repoBranch.description: Legacy shorthand for sites.default.repoBranch. Git ref appended to GitHub source URLs (branch / tag / commit SHA). Defaults to `main`. Use a commit SHA for archival URLs that won't drift when the file is edited later.

dir.description: Plan directory, relative to project root. Default: 'plan'

theme.description: Active theme — accepts a package name string (legacy shorthand) or a full SiteThemeConfig with presets, token overrides, mode overlays, and code-block colour scheme.

target.description: Documentation-only adapter hint (svelte, astro, next, nuxt, eleventy, html). No adapter validates or reads this field today; it serves as an in-config record of the intended adapter. Slated for removal in v1.0.

plugins.description: Plugins to merge into this site's ThemeConfig (runes, layouts, hooks, etc.).

backgrounds.description: Named bg presets (keyed by name). Each is a BgPresetDefinition: `style` and/or token-driven `gradient` (engine-resolved), `extends` a base preset, and/or `sandbox` ({ src, framework?, dependencies? }) for a live sandbox backdrop resolved at transform time (SPEC-104). `sandbox` presets are project-level content.

repoUrl.description: Canonical GitHub (or compatible) repository URL — e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo`. Used by the `file-ref` rune (SPEC-078) to build deep-link `View source on GitHub →` URLs of the form `{repoUrl}/blob/{repoBranch}/{path}#L{start}-L{end}`. When absent, `file-ref` falls back to a no-href link / in-page anchor with a build warning.

repoBranch.description: Git ref appended to GitHub source URLs — accepts any branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to `main` when omitted. Use a commit SHA for archival URLs that won't drift when the file is edited later.

HighlightConfig.description: Legacy syntax-highlighting configuration — picks a Shiki built-in theme by name (or a `{ light, dark }` pair) for fenced code blocks. The recommended modern approach is `theme.presets` (which contribute `--rf-syntax-*` token overrides via Lumina syntax presets like `@refrakt-md/lumina/presets/nord`) plus `theme.code.colorScheme` for forced light/dark code blocks. Kept for back-compat; both mechanisms can coexist.

theme.description: Built-in Shiki theme name (e.g. `github-dark`), or a `{ light, dark }` pair for dual-theme support that switches with the site's light/dark mode.

SiteThemeConfig.description: Per-site theme configuration with token overrides, presets, modes, and code-block colour-scheme behaviour. The legacy string form (`"theme": "@refrakt-md/lumina"`) is shorthand for `{ "package": "@refrakt-md/lumina" }`.

package.description: Active theme — npm package name (e.g., `@refrakt-md/lumina`) or relative path.

presets.description: Preset modules merged into the theme in declared order (last wins per token). Each entry is a module identifier (npm package + export path) resolving to a token preset — e.g. `@refrakt-md/lumina/presets/niwaki` for palette presets, `@refrakt-md/lumina/presets/nord` for syntax-colour presets.

colorScheme.description: Initial colour scheme behaviour for the rendered site. `auto` (default) respects user preference (saved or system); `light` / `dark` force the scheme regardless of preference.

tokens.description: Site-level token overrides applied on top of the theme's base values and any presets. Validated against the theme's TokenContract at build time. Nested by token category (e.g. `color`, `space`, `radius`).

modes.description: Site-level per-mode overlays (e.g. `dark`). Layer on top of the theme's modes and any preset modes. Keys are mode names; values are partial token contracts.

colorScheme.description: Force fenced code blocks to a fixed colour scheme regardless of the page's light/dark mode. `auto` (default) lets code follow the page mode; `light` / `dark` stamp `data-color-scheme` on each `<pre>` so the cascade picks that scheme's syntax token values — useful for an always-dark code aesthetic on light pages (Stripe / Vercel style) without a parallel override stylesheet.
